question_title: Will Anthropic make Claude 5 available via a public API before May 1, 2026?
summary: As of March 9, 2026, no Claude 5 or Claude 5.x model exists in Anthropic's official API documentation. The latest models are Claude Opus 4.6 (Feb 5) and Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Feb 17). While there was a leaked 'claude-sonnet-5' identifier (codename Fennec) in Vertex AI logs, Anthropic released models under 4.6 naming instead. The community consensus places Claude 5 in Q2-Q3 2026 (May-September), making a release before May 1 at the extreme early edge of forecasts. Manifold gives 37% for 'before June 1' and only 6% for 'Sonnet 5 in March.' Adjusting the June 1 market for the shorter 7-week window to May 1 suggests roughly 15%. Anthropic's rapid release cadence and competitive pressure from GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3 create a nonzero but modest probability of a surprise April launch.

factors.description: No Claude 5 or Claude 5.x model exists in Anthropic's official API documentation as of March 9, 2026. Status quo = NO.
Anthropic just released two major 4.6 models (Opus 4.6 on Feb 5, Sonnet 4.6 on Feb 17) — a major version jump to 5.0 within 2 months of those releases is unusually fast even by current cadence.
Manifold market 'Will Claude 5 be released before June 1?' at 37%. Before May 1 (7 weeks shorter window) implies roughly 15-22% via distribution reasoning.
Manifold market 'Will Anthropic release Claude Sonnet 5 in March 2026?' at only 6%, indicating near-term release is unlikely.
Leaked 'Fennec' / claude-sonnet-5 identifier in Vertex AI logs suggests model may be in development/testing, creating a nonzero chance of surprise release in April.
Intense competitive pressure from OpenAI GPT-5.4 and Google Gemini 3 could push Anthropic to accelerate release schedule.
Anthropic dealing with Pentagon blacklist, government lawsuit, and political controversy — potential distraction from product launches.
Community/analyst consensus places Claude 5 in Q2-Q3 2026 (May-September), with earliest reasonable estimate around May. Before May 1 is at the extreme early edge.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 15% probability** that Anthropic makes Claude 5 available via a public API before May 1, 2026.

**Key reasoning:**
- **No Claude 5 exists today**: Official Anthropic docs list only Claude Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, and Haiku 4.5
- **Recent releases make a quick jump unlikely**: Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 were just released in February 2026 — a major version jump within ~2 months is unusually fast
- **Market calibration**: Manifold gives 37% for "Claude 5 before June 1" — adjusting for the shorter May 1 window gives ~15-20%
- **Small upside path**: The leaked Fennec/Sonnet 5 identifier suggests the model may be in late-stage development, and competitive pressure from GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3 could push Anthropic to surprise-launch in April
- **Downside risks**: Anthropic is embroiled in government controversies (Pentagon blacklist, lawsuit), potentially distracting from product launches

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if, before May 1, 2026, Anthropic announces a model named Claude 5 (or Claude 5.x) and Anthropic’s official API documentation shows that this model can be called via a publicly available API (not limited to a private beta or invite-only access). This question resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is Anthropic’s documentation at https://docs.anthropic.com/
fine_print: “Public API” means generally available access under standard Anthropic account terms (it may still require creating an account and obtaining an API key, but not an individualized invite-only program). If the model name is ambiguous, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions while prioritizing Anthropic docs and official announcements. Candidate Sources: Anthropic news https://www.anthropic.com/news
background_info:
Public API availability determines whether developers can deploy the model in products at scale.
